title: RnS terminal-session suppression (IDEA-0009 / EXP-0001)
description: Run IDEA-0009's smallest safe experiment (EXP-0001) and land the fix: the limit watcher treats agents whose durable state records inactive/session_superseded or inactive/disposable_session_ended as terminal — no probes, no presumed-down incidents, no resume/check-in/work nudges — and closes existing open incidents for terminal agents. Every suppression must be visible in dry-run output (IDEA-0009 reversibility condition). Lifecycle marks are written through SQLite agents table first, then exported (SYN-0001 rule), for the 8 genuinely-dead early-July sessions currently carrying gave-up incidents. Baseline and post-change --once --dry-run outputs are captured as experiment evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Watcher helper classifies inactive/session_superseded and inactive/disposable_session_ended as terminal; terminal agents get no probes, incidents, check-ins, or work nudges.
Open incidents for now-terminal agents are closed with an explicit closed_reason and surfaced in dry-run output as suppressions, not silently dropped.
Lifecycle marks written via SQLite-first path (extended declare_standby.py or equivalent), then exported; status.json never hand-edited.
Before/after --once --dry-run outputs captured in the evidence artifact; after-run shows zero probe attempts for the 8 dead sessions and visible suppression lines.
Focused tests cover terminal classification, incident closure, and dry-run visibility; full run_tests.sh stays green.
EXP-0001 updated with results and IDEA-0009 lifecycle closed out per its Test recommendation.
